metadata.summary: A package to run bots for 漢字でGO!.

documentation:
# kadego

`kadego` is a python package for running bots to play the browser game [漢字でGO!](https://plicy.net/GamePlay/155561).

This package allows you to automate gameplay, collect data (like readings and meanings), and customize game-playing strategies.

## 📦 Structure

This package provides five main objects to work with: `Bot`, `Logger`, `Observer`, `Strategy` and `Runner`.

### 🦾 Bot

The `Bot` is the one actually playing the game.

When calling its `launch` method it navigates to the provided `mode` and `level`, sets the provided number of `lives` and `questions` and finally enters into the `level`. Calling the `run` method will then make the bot play a full game answering questions according to the specified `strategy` and logging answers with the specified `logger`.

### 📜 Logger

A `Logger` logs data from the game.

Its `log` method is called each time an answer is presented to the associated `Bot` and provided with the current `gameVariables`. An example use case would be storing readings and meanings to a JSON file.

### 👀 Observer

An `Observer` runs in parallel to a `Bot`.

Its `observe` method runs continuously and can perform actions depending on the state of the `Bot` or any outside factors. One of these actions can be stopping the `Runner` from continuing to run the `Bot`. An example use case would be stopping the `Bot` after a certain amount of time or when the intended task has been completed.

### 🧠 Strategy

A `Strategy` returns an answer and the corresponding outcome when presented with a question.

Its `solve` method is called each time the associated `Bot` is presented with a question. The `Strategy` will then depending on the `gameVariables` and the current `GameStatus` choose a `Solver` to provide an answer and the associated outcome. An example use case would be acing the game by always choosing the right answer.

### 🚀 Runner

A `Runner` is responsible for concurrently running a `Bot` together with an `Observer`.

Its `run` method runs the specified `Bot` by first calling its `launch` method and the repeatedly calling its `run` method until a signal to stop is sent. Additionally, it also runs the specified `Observer` by calling its `observe` method.

## 🗺️ Map

The different game modes are conceptualized as a `WorldMap` which the `Bot` uses to navigate namely the `KanjiDeGoMap`.

### ⚙️ Main

The main (メイン) `mode` has `levels`:
- easy (ノーマル)
- hard (ハード)
- very_hard (ゲキムズ)
- hell (ヘル)

### 🔄 Casual

The casual (カジュアル) `mode` has `levels`:
- basic (ベーシック)
- variety (バラエティ)
- variety_hard (鬼・バラエティ)
- one_character (一文字)
- four_characters (四字熟語)
- places (国・都市の名前)
- bugs (虫の名前)
- mammals (哺乳類の名前)
- birds (鳥の名前)
- aquatic (水性物の名前)
- plants (植物の名前)
- food (グルメ・調味料)

### ⏱️ Rush

The rush (ラッシュ) `mode` has `levels`:
- level_1 (１ラッシュ)
- level_2 (２ラッシュ)
- level_3 (３ラッシュ)
- level_4 (４ラッシュ)
- level_5 (５ラッシュ)
- level_6 (６ラッシュ)

### 🌟 Extra
extra (エクストラ) `mode` has `levels`:
- math (数学アタック)
- math_hard (鬼・数学アタック)
- english (英語・アタック)
- elements (元素記号)

## ⚙️ Installation

To install kadego, run
```bash
pip install kadego
```

## 📚 Example Usage

To run a bot that scrapes all the readings and meanings for words from a single level, you can run the following.
````python
import asyncio
from kadego import get_kadego
from kadego.runners import SingleLevelScraper


async def run(level: int, log_path: str) -> None:
    kadego = await get_kadego()
    runner = SingleLevelScraper(kadego, level, log_path)

    await runner.run()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    example_level = 1
    exampe_log_path = "/path/to/store/data/to"
    asyncio.run(run(example_level, exampe_log_path))
````
The bot will then run until the data for all words has been collected.

## 🛠️ Troubleshooting

`漢字でGO!` might crash or time out from time to time. This will in turn crash the `Bot` meaning you wil need to rerun your script.

## 📝 Changelog

v0.1.0 — Initial release (based on 漢字でGO! v1.1.2.2b).

modules.name: kadego.observers.periodic_observer
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.observers.periodic_observer.PeriodicObserver

methods.name: kadego.observers.periodic_observer.PeriodicObserver.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the observer given a bot and delay.

Args:
    bot (Bot): The bot to be observed.
    delay (float, optional): The delay (in seconds) between each observation cycle (default is 0.2).

methods.name: kadego.observers.periodic_observer.PeriodicObserver._loop_function
documentation:
Performs the actual observation

Args:
    stop_event (asyncio.Event): Event that when set stops the runner from running the bot.

methods.name: kadego.observers.periodic_observer.PeriodicObserver.observe
documentation:
Runs the function performing the actual observation in a loop.

Args:
    stop_event (asyncio.Event): Event that when set stops the runner from running the bot.

An abstract base class where the observation is performed periodically.

This `Observer` performs the observation in a loop periodically waiting
for a specified amount of time.

modules.name: kadego.observers.progress_bar_observer
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.observers.progress_bar_observer.ProgressBarObserver

methods.name: kadego.observers.progress_bar_observer.ProgressBarObserver.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the observer given a bot and delay.

Args:
    bot (Bot): The bot to be observed.
    delay (float, optional): The delay (in seconds) between each observation cycle (default is 0.2).

methods.name: kadego.observers.progress_bar_observer.ProgressBarObserver._loop_function
documentation:
Updates the progress bar based on the bot's logger data and stops
the `Runner` from running the `Bot` once all words' data has been
collected.

Args:
    stop_event (asyncio.Event): Event that when set stops the runner from running the bot.

An `Observer` priting the current status of a `Bot` as well as the
progress via a progress bar.

This `Observer` is meant to work together with a `Bot` that has an
instance of `SingleLevelWordsLogger` as its `Logger`. A progress bar
is printed that shows how many distinct words' data was already
collected and updates periodically. Once all words' data hase been
collected the `Runner` will be stopped from running the `Bot`.

modules.name: kadego.observers.observer
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.observers.observer.Observer

methods.name: kadego.observers.observer.Observer.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the observer given a bot.

Args:
    bot (Bot): The bot to be observed.

methods.name: kadego.observers.observer.Observer.observe
documentation:
Observes the bot reacting to changes or outside factors.

Args:
    stop_event (asyncio.Event): Event that when set stops the runner from running the bot.

An Abstract base class for observing a bot.

An `Observer` runs in parallel to a `Bot` reacting to changes in its
state or outside factors e.g. by stopping the `Runner` from running
the `Bot`.

modules.name: kadego.observers.status_observer
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.observers.status_observer.StatusObserver

methods.name: kadego.observers.status_observer.StatusObserver._loop_function
documentation:
Prints the current state of the bot.

Args:
    stop_event (asyncio.Event): Event that when set stops the runner from running the bot.

An `Observer` priting the current status of a `Bot`.

This `Observer` prints the current state of a `Bot` periodically
overwriting it.

classes.name: kadego.exceptions.invalid_bot_configuration_error.InvalidBotConfigurationError
documentation: Exception raised when the bot configuration is invalid.

modules.name: kadego.observers.timeout_observer
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.observers.timeout_observer.TimeOutObserver

methods.name: kadego.observers.timeout_observer.TimeOutObserver.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the observer given a bot and delay.

Args:
    bot (Bot): The bot to be observed.
    timeout (float): The time (in seconds) after which the bot will be stopped from being run.
    delay (float, optional): The delay (in seconds) between each observation cycle (default is 0.2).

methods.name: kadego.observers.timeout_observer.TimeOutObserver._loop_function
documentation:
Stops the bot from being run if the timeout was exceeded.

Args:
    stop_event (asyncio.Event): Event that when set stops the runner from running the bot.

An `Observer` that stops the `Bot` from being run after a specified
amount of time.

This `Observer` periodically checks how long a `Bot` has been running for
already. If this time exceeds some specified threshold the `Runner` will
be stopped from running the `Bot`.

classes.name: kadego.exceptions.incompatible_bot_error.IncompatibleBotError
documentation: Exception raised when an observer is incompatible with the its bot.

modules.name: kadego.solvers.solver
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.solvers.solver.Solver

methods.name: kadego.solvers.solver.Solver.__init__
documentation: Initializes the solver.

methods.name: kadego.solvers.solver.Solver.solve
documentation:
Chooses an answer and an outcome based on provided game variables.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of the chosen answer and the corresponding outcome.

An abstract base class for solving a question.

A `Solver` chooses an answer and a corresponding `QuestionOutcome`
given the game variables.

modules.name: kadego.solvers.correct_solver
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.solvers.correct_solver.CorrectSolver

methods.name: kadego.solvers.correct_solver.CorrectSolver.solve
documentation:
Chooses the correct answer to the question.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of the correct answer and the 'Correct' outcome.
A `Solver` that always chooses the correct answer.

modules.name: kadego.solvers.skip_solver
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.solvers.skip_solver.SkipSolver

methods.name: kadego.solvers.skip_solver.SkipSolver.solve
documentation:
Chooses to skip the question.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of an empty string and the 'Skipped' outcome.
A `Solver` that always chooses to skip.

modules.name: kadego.solvers.quit_solver
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.solvers.quit_solver.QuitSolver

methods.name: kadego.solvers.quit_solver.QuitSolver.solve
documentation:
Chooses to quit the question.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of 'q' and the 'Incorrect' outcome.
A `Solver` that always chooses to quit.

modules.name: kadego.world_maps.kanji_de_go_map
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.world_maps.kanji_de_go_map.KanjiDeGoMap

methods.name: kadego.world_maps.kanji_de_go_map.KanjiDeGoMap.__init__
documentation: Initializes the WorldMap of 漢字でGO!
A `WorldMap` of 漢字でGO!.

modules.name: kadego.strategies.strategy
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.strategies.strategy.Strategy

methods.name: kadego.strategies.strategy.Strategy.__init__
documentation: Initializes the strategy.

methods.name: kadego.strategies.strategy.Strategy.get_solver
documentation:
Chooses the appropriate solver based on the game variables and
current game status.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.
    status (GameStatus): The current game status (e.g. number of lives)

Returns:
    Solver: The choosen solver to answer the question.

methods.name: kadego.strategies.strategy.Strategy.solve
documentation:
Solves the question by getting an answer from the appropriate solver.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.
    status (GameStatus): The current game status (e.g. number of lives)

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of the chosen answer and the corresponding outcome.

An abstract base class for solving questions base on the current game
status.

A `Strategy` answers questions getting an answer from an appropriate
`Solver` based on the current game status.

modules.name: kadego.strategies.always_quit_strategy
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.strategies.always_quit_strategy.AlwaysQuitStrategy

methods.name: kadego.strategies.always_quit_strategy.AlwaysQuitStrategy.get_solver
documentation:
Chooses to quit the question.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.
    status (GameStatus): The current game status (e.g. number of lives)

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of 'q' and the 'Incorrect' outcome.

A `Strategy` that always chooses the `QuitSolver` to answer
questions.

classes.name: kadego.world_maps.world_map.WorldMap

methods.name: kadego.world_maps.world_map.WorldMap.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the WorldMap.

Args:
    modes (Modes): A dictionary specifying the mode locations.
    mode_levels (ModeLevels): A dictionary specifying the level locations.
    popups (Popups, optional): A list of popups (default is list()).
    mode_popups (ModePopups, optional): A dictionary specifying the mode popups (default is dict()).
    mode_level_popups (ModeLevelPopups, optional): A dictionary specifying the level popups (default is dict()).

methods.name: kadego.world_maps.world_map.WorldMap.get_mode_direction
documentation:
Retrieves the location of a mode.

Args:
    mode (str): The mode for which the location is requested.

Returns:
    int: The amount of right arrow button presses necessary to get to the location.

methods.name: kadego.world_maps.world_map.WorldMap.get_level_direction
documentation:
Retrieves the location of a level in a mode.

Args:
    mode (str): The mode of the given level.
    level (str): The level for which the location is requested.

Returns:
    int: The amount of right arrow button presses necessary to get to the level.

methods.name: kadego.world_maps.world_map.WorldMap.get_popups
documentation:
Gives instructions on how to close popups.

Returns:
    Popups: The list of button presses necessary to close the popups.

methods.name: kadego.world_maps.world_map.WorldMap.get_mode_popups
documentation:
Gives instructions on how to close popups in a mode.

Args:
    mode (str): The mode in which the popups appear.

Returns:
    Popups: The list of button presses necessary to close the popups.

methods.name: kadego.world_maps.world_map.WorldMap.get_mode_level_popups
documentation:
Gives instructions on how to close popups in a level of a mode.

Args:
    mode (str): The mode of the given level.
    level (str): The level in which the popups appear.

Returns:
    Popups: The list of button presses necessary to close the popups.

A class representing a world map of a game.

A `WorldMap` consists of different modes which in turn have levels. Each of
these has a location which represents the amount of right arrow button
presses it would take to get there. Additionally, the game in general, each
mode and each level in a mode can have a number of popups which appear.

modules.name: kadego.strategies.always_correct_strategy
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.strategies.always_correct_strategy.AlwaysCorrectStrategy

methods.name: kadego.strategies.always_correct_strategy.AlwaysCorrectStrategy.get_solver
documentation:
Chooses the correct answer to the question.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.
    status (GameStatus): The current game status (e.g. number of lives)

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of the correct answer and the 'Correct' outcome.

A `Strategy` that always chooses the `CorrectSolver` to answer
questions.

modules.name: kadego.strategies.curious_strategy
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.strategies.curious_strategy.CuriousStrategy

methods.name: kadego.strategies.curious_strategy.CuriousStrategy.get_solver
documentation:
Selects to skip when possible and answer correctly otherwise. That
is except for at the peniultimate question where it will select to
quit until only one life remaining and on the last question where it
will also choose to quit after skippingis no longer possible.

Args:
     game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a question was posed.
     status (GameStatus): The current game status (e.g. number of lives)

 Returns:
     tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: A tuple of the chosen answer and the corresponding outcome.
A `Strategy` aims to maximize the amount of questions in a round.

modules.name: kadego.runners.single_level_scrapper
documentation: Third-Party Imports

classes.name: kadego.runners.single_level_scrapper.SingleLevelScraper

methods.name: kadego.runners.single_level_scrapper.SingleLevelScraper.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the runner with all its components.

Args:
    kadego (Page): The Playwright page for playing 漢字でGO!.
    level (int): The level to be scraped for words.
    path (str): The file path where the scraped data will be stored.

A runner for scraping the word data of individual levels from 漢字でGO!.

This class sets up a `Bot` configured to play the 'Rush' mode with the
`CuriousStrategy` as well as a `SingleLevelWordsLogger` to log the
scraped words and a `ProgressBarObserver` to display the progress.

modules.name: kadego.runners.runner
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.runners.runner.Runner

methods.name: kadego.runners.runner.Runner.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the runner with a bot and an optional observer.

Args:
    bot (Bot): The bot that will be launched and executed by the Runner.
    observer (Observer): The observer for the bot.

methods.name: kadego.runners.runner.Runner.run
documentation:
Starts running the bot in a loop as well as the observer.

This method launches the bot and starts monitoring the bot's
activity. The bot will continue running in a loop until the
observer sends a signal to stop.

A class responsible for running a bot playing 漢字でGO!

A `Runner` can launch a `Bot` and an `Observer` to monitor the bot's
activity.

modules.name: kadego.loggers.base_logger
documentation: Local Imports

classes.name: kadego.loggers.base_logger.BaseLogger

methods.name: kadego.loggers.base_logger.BaseLogger.__init__
documentation: Initializes the logger.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.base_logger.BaseLogger.log
documentation:
Does not do anything.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a solution was revealed.
A basic `Logger` that does not do actual logging

modules.name: kadego.loggers.logger
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.loggers.logger.Logger

methods.name: kadego.loggers.logger.Logger.__init__
documentation: Initializes the logger.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.logger.Logger.log
documentation:
Logs the given game variables.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a solution was revealed.

An Abstract base class for logging game variables.

A `Logger` is used by a `Bot` to log game data. Each time the solution
to a question is revealed the `Bot` will call the `Logger` to log the
current game variables.

modules.name: kadego.utils.question_outcome
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.utils.question_outcome.QuestionOutcome
documentation:
A class representing the outcome of a question being answered.

Attributes:
    correct (bool): Indicates whether the question was answered correctly.
    skipped (bool): Indicates whether the question was skipped.

modules.name: kadego.utils.playwright
documentation: Standard Library Imports

functions.name: kadego.utils.playwright._launch_browser
documentation:
Launches a Chromium browser instance using Playwright and returns a
page.

Args:
    headless (bool): Whether to launch the browser in headless mode (without UI).

Returns:
    Page: A Playwright Page object representing the newly opened browser page.

functions.name: kadego.utils.playwright.get_kadego
documentation:
Opens 漢字でGO! in a new browser instance and returns the page.

Args:
    headless (bool, optional): Whether to run the browser in headless mode (default is True).

Returns:
    Page: A Playwright Page object representing the page after opening 漢字でGO!.

functions.name: kadego.utils.playwright.press_key
documentation:
Simulates pressing a key on the keyboard.

Args:
    page (Page): The Playwright Page object.
    key (str): The key to press.

functions.name: kadego.utils.playwright.press_key_sequence
documentation:
Simulates pressing a sequence of keys on the keyboard.

Args:
    page (Page): The Playwright Page object.
    keys (Sequence[str]): The sequence of keys to press.
    delay (float): The delay (in seconds) between key presses

functions.name: kadego.utils.playwright.get_game_variables
documentation:
Retrieves the current game variables from a page.

Args:
    page (Page): The Playwright Page object.

Returns:
    game_variables (dict): The current game variables.

documentation:
Initializes the WordData.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a solution was revealed.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.single_level_words_logger.WordData._is_reading
documentation:
Checks if the given reading is not a dummy.

Args:
    reading (str): The reading to be checked.

Returns:
    bool: True if the reading is not a dummy, False if it is a dummy.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.single_level_words_logger.WordData._filter_reading
documentation:
Filters unwanted characters from a reading.

Args:
    reading (str): The reading string to be filtered.

Returns:
    str: The filtered reading with unwanted characters removed.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.single_level_words_logger.WordData._is_meaning
documentation:
Checks if the given meaning is not a dummy.

Args:
    meaning (str): The meaning to be checked.

Returns:
    bool: True if the meaning is not a dummy, False if it is a dummy.

A class to process and store word-related data extracted from game
variables.

This class extracts and filters relevant word readings and meanings from
the provided game data.

Initializes the logger.

Args:

    path (str): The path to the JSON file where log data will be stored.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.single_level_words_logger.SingleLevelWordsLogger.log
documentation:
Logs the readings and meanings from the given game variables.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a solution was revealed.

A `Logger` logs data for a single level into a JSON file.

play through rounds by answering questions using the specified `Strategy`.
This `Logger` is a `JsonFileLogger` that stores readings and meanings of
question coming from a single level.

Attributes:
    questions_for_levels (dict[int, int]): The number of questions for the levels in 漢字でGO!.

Initializes the logger.

Args:
    path (str): The path to the JSON file where log data will be stored.

documentation:
Reads data from the specified JSON file.

Returns:
    dict: The data read from the JSON file.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.json_file_logger.JsonFileLogger._write_data
documentation: Writes the current log data to the specified JSON file.

methods.name: kadego.loggers.json_file_logger.JsonFileLogger.log
documentation:
Logs the given game variables to be written into a JSON file.

Args:
    game_variables (dict): The game variables shortly after a solution was revealed.

An Abstract base class for logging game variables into a JSON file.

This `Logger` temporarily stores the data from the game variables and
writes it into a JSON file.

modules.name: kadego.utils.game_status
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.utils.game_status.GameStatus

methods.name: kadego.utils.game_status.GameStatus.update
documentation:
Updates the game status based on the outcome of a question.

Args:
    outcome (QuestionOutcome): The outcome of the question

methods.name: kadego.utils.game_status.GameStatus.is_done
documentation:
Checks if the round is over.

Returns:
    bool: True if not lives remain or the total number of questions was reached, False otherwise.

methods.name: kadego.utils.game_status.GameStatus.is_win
documentation:
Checks if the round was one.

Returns:
    bool: True if the round is done and lives still remain, False otherwise.

methods.name: kadego.utils.game_status.GameStatus.is_loss
documentation:
Checks if the round was one.

Returns:
    bool: True if the round is done and not lives remain, False otherwise.

A class representing the current status of the game and updates
when given a `QuestionOutcome`.

Attributes:
    lives (int): The number of lives remaining.
    questions (int): The total number of questions.
    question (int): The current question number.
    skip (bool): Flag indicating whether skipping is possible.

modules.name: kadego.bots.bot
documentation: Standard Library Imports

classes.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the bot with necessary parameters.

Args:
    kadego (Page): The Playwright page for playing 漢字でGO!.
    mode (str): The mode to play.
    level (str): The level to play.
    strategy (Strategy): The strategy for answering questions.
    lives (int, optional): The number of lives to set.
    questions (int, optional): The number of questions to set (default is 16).
    logger (Logger, optional): The logger to use (default is `BaseLogger`).
    world_map (WorldMap, optional): The world map to use (default is `KanjiDeGoMap`).
    speed (float, optional): The speed multiplier for bot actions (default is 1.0).

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._validate
documentation: Check if the bot's configuration is valid.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._get_new_game_status
documentation:
Returns a new game status object based on the specified lives and
questions.

Returns:
    GameStatus: A new game status object.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._wait
documentation:
Pauses the bot for a specified amount of time, scaled by the bot's
speed.

Args:
    delay (float): The time to wait in seconds.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._start_game
documentation: Starts the game.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._click_through_popups
documentation: Clicks through the popups at the start of the game.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._navigate_to_mode
documentation: Navigates to the specified mode.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._enter_mode
documentation: Enters the specified mode.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._click_through_mode_popups
documentation: Clicks through the popups of the mode.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._navigate_to_level
documentation: Navigates to the specified level.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._set_lives
documentation: Sets the number of lives.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._enter_level
documentation: Enters the specified level.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._click_through_level_popups
documentation: Clicks through the popups of the level.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._set_questions
documentation: Sets the number of questions.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._start_round
documentation: Starts a round.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._wait_for_round_to_load
documentation:
Waits for some time for the round to load.

Returns:
    bool: True if the round loaded in time, False if it　did not.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._wait_for_question
documentation: Waits for the next question.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._get_answer
documentation:
Retrieves an answer to the current question using the selected
strategy.

Returns:
    tuple[str, QuestionOutcome]: The answer and the　outcome of the question.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._answer_question
documentation:
Provides an answer to the current question and updates the game
status according to the provided outcome.

Args:
    answer (str): The answer to submit.
    outcome　(QuestionOutcome): The outcome of the question.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._wait_for_solution
documentation:
Waits for the solution to be displayed and logs the current game
state.

Args:
    outcome (QuestionOutcome): The outcome of the question.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._wait_for_round_reload_loop
documentation: Waits for a new round to start, reloading the game if stuck.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._answer_questions_loop
documentation:
Waits for and answers questions in a loop until the game is
finished.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._wait_for_round_to_finish
documentation:
Waits a little at the end of a round for e.g. the boss defeated
animation to finish.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot._retry
documentation: Starts a new round by retrying.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot.launch
documentation:
Navigates through the game performing all necessary setup and
finally starts a round.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot.reload
documentation: Reloads the game and starts a new round again.

methods.name: kadego.bots.bot.Bot.run
documentation: Runs the bot for one round of play.

A class for automating the process of playing 漢字でGO!.

A `Bot` can navigate to a given mode and level using a given `WorldMap`
and set the number of lives and questions accordingly. It can also then
play through rounds by answering questions using the specified `Strategy`.
Additonally, question data can be logged by a given `Logger`.
